summary: Wake-cycle complete. All 3 students delivered cycle 1 and assigned cycle 2 experiments. Golden's taxonomy: 60-70% harness-fixable. 7 hypotheses, 3 being tested. Experiments running: spec ambiguity (HongYang 6 subagents), feedback quality (Matheus assigned), context injection (Golden assigned).

dynamic_hooks.description: commit all cycle work before stopping

key_facts: Golden taxonomy: 60-70% of 24.2pp METR gap is harness/prompting-fixable
Code quality = 14-16pp of gap, primarily an elicitation problem, not capability
Model progression: newer models improve elicitation sensitivity, not reasoning
Time horizon 7x overstatement: 50min auto vs 8min human
H7 (NEW): benchmark increasingly measures elicitation skill, not model capability
Three experiments running: H3 (context injection, Golden), H5 (spec ambiguity, HongYang), H6 (feedback quality, Matheus)
OpenDev paper: instruction fade-out countered by event-driven reminders
Industry converging on harness engineering: OpenAI, Anthropic, Martin Fowler all publishing
If H5+H6 validate, enough for paper: Diagnostic Benchmark for Agent Harnesses
All 3 students + hongyang-assist active on non-overlapping tasks
